summary.intent: Implement a Nostr DM channel for the Moltis messaging platform, achieving feature parity with existing channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) including inbound/outbound DMs, OTP access control, metrics, NIP-44 support, web UI, onboarding, docs, and integration tests.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the moltis-nostr crate (~2,200 LOC across 12 source files) with plugin lifecycle, NIP-04/44 encryption, relay subscription loop, OTP pairing flow, metrics instrumentation, web UI add/edit/onboarding flows, Playwright E2E tests, documentation, and passing integration tests against real Nostr relays. All CI checks pass.

repo: Channel plugins follow a strict pattern: ChannelType variant in crates/channels/src/plugin.rs, optional dep in gateway/Cargo.toml, feature-gated registration in crates/gateway/src/server.rs, and named field in crates/config/src/schema.rs
ChannelType is #[non_exhaustive] so exhaustive match arms in gateway/channel.rs use wildcard _ and don't need updating for new variants
ALL constant in plugin.rs has a test verifying count matches discriminant count — must be updated when adding variants
Config named_fields() and all_channel_configs() must be kept in sync; new channels should be added as named fields (not just via flatten/extra)

OTP flow requires: OtpState shared as Arc<Mutex<OtpState>>, bus calls otp.initiate() to generate code, sends code DM via outbound client, then emits OtpChallenge event — not just a placeholder event emit
Biome formatter (via cargo +nightly fmt) enforces method chain line-breaking for chains of 3+ calls on a single expression
The website/ locale files (10 locales) were already pre-populated with Nostr references; only the SVG icon was missing
Website icons use fill='currentColor' for light/dark mode compatibility
CI runs local/biome check that catches JS formatting issues not caught by biome check alone (different formatter version/config)
The moltis branch history used worktrees with unrelated histories to origin/main; cherry-pick onto main base was required instead of merge

code.finding: ALL constant has a test asserting count == enum discriminant count; must update both ALL array and test when adding ChannelType variants
named_fields() must list every named channel field; Default impl must initialize them; all_channel_configs() iterates both named fields and extra (serde flatten)
String truncation at byte boundary — use str::floor_char_boundary(MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES) not direct slice; plain indexing panics on multi-byte UTF-8
RwLockReadGuard must not be held across .await points; extract needed values into locals before any await
Config must be Arc<RwLock<NostrAccountConfig>> shared between AccountState and the bus loop task, not cloned at spawn time; otherwise live config updates don't reach the running bus
update_account_config must check for [REDACTED] sentinel in secret_key before merging to prevent corrupting the stored key with the redacted display value
notifications() broadcast receiver must be created BEFORE connect()/subscribe() to avoid missing early relay messages; getting it after connect causes race where EOSE arrives before receiver exists

workflow: nostr-sdk v0.44: nip04 and nip44 features are NOT in default features — must be explicitly enabled in workspace Cargo.toml
nostr-sdk prelude imports nip44::{self, *} which brings decrypt into scope directly, conflicting with nip04::decrypt — use fully qualified nostr_sdk::nips::nip44::decrypt to avoid ambiguity lint
nostr-sdk Client::subscribe() takes a single Filter not Vec<Filter>; disconnect() returns () not Result
nostr-sdk PublicKey::to_bech32() and SecretKey::to_bech32() return Result<String, Infallible> — must .unwrap() or .unwrap_or_default()
Metadata::picture() takes Url type not String — parse first with Url::parse()
taplo fmt reorders TOML keys alphabetically and reformats; running it causes cosmetic diffs in unrelated Cargo.toml files
For optional deps in Cargo features, use dep?/feature syntax (e.g., moltis-nostr?/metrics) to avoid enabling the optional dep when the parent feature is on but the dep feature isn't
gh secret set for CI integration test keys: gh secret set KEY_NAME --body 'value'

friction: nostr-sdk nip04/nip44 features not in defaults caused compile failures that required iterative discovery
nostr-sdk prelude glob import creates name collisions (nip04::decrypt vs nip44::decrypt) that trigger confusing 'unnecessary qualification' lint errors
Branch had unrelated history to origin/main (worktree setup), making git merge impossible without --allow-unrelated-histories which caused 70+ conflicts; required abort and cherry-pick strategy
DM round-trip integration test initially failed due to notifications() receiver being created after connect() — race condition took multiple iterations to diagnose
PR review caught that OTP flow was wired but non-functional (emitted placeholder event without actually initiating challenge or sending code DM) — required significant bus/state/plugin refactor
matrix accidentally promoted from extra/flatten to named field in ChannelsConfig as a side-effect of adding nostr — required a separate revert commit
Biome JS formatter enforces method chain line-breaking that differs from what was written, caught only in CI not locally
open_items: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs (more privacy, less widely supported) — explicitly deferred to future PR
Metrics instrumentation exists but no counters/histograms are wired yet for relay health (connected/disconnected relay count gauge only)
Contract test suite (moltis_channels::contract) not run against NostrPlugin — requires valid credentials to start so incompatible with current contract test design
Outbound NIP-44 (currently inbound-only NIP-44 decryption; outbound defaults to NIP-04) — config option to prefer NIP-44 outbound noted but not implemented
Integration tests require NOSTR_TEST_BOT_KEY and NOSTR_TEST_SENDER_KEY GitHub secrets to be added for CI to run them non-ignored
